title: The Punic Wars
description: A series of three wars fought between Rome and Carthage for dominance of the western Mediterranean. Rome's eventual victory established it as the preeminent power in the region.

title: Assassination of Julius Caesar
description: The murder of Julius Caesar by a group of senators marked the end of the Roman Republic and triggered a chain of civil wars that gave rise to the Roman Empire.

title: Pax Romana
description: A period of relative peace and stability across the Roman Empire lasting roughly 200 years, enabling trade, infrastructure, and cultural flourishing across Europe and the Mediterranean.

title: Fall of the Western Roman Empire
description: The deposition of Romulus Augustulus by Odoacer marked the conventional end of the Western Roman Empire, fragmenting Europe into successor kingdoms.

title: Mongol Conquests
description: Under Genghis Khan, the Mongols built the largest contiguous land empire in history, reshaping trade routes, spreading plague, and toppling established civilizations from China to Persia.

title: The Black Death
description: The bubonic plague killed an estimated one-third of Europe's population, destabilizing feudal structures, accelerating labor reforms, and indirectly fueling the conditions for the Renaissance.

title: The Italian Renaissance
description: A cultural and intellectual rebirth centered in Florence, Venice, and Rome. Humanism, art, and science flourished, drawing inspiration from classical antiquity and reshaping European civilization.
